# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: kubia-service
  labels:
    app: kubia
    version: v2
spec:
  ports:
  - port: 443 # HTTPS service port (for internal use only)
    targetPort: 8000 # TCP port of the app container to expose (default HTTP port).
    protocol: TCP # this must be set as a protocol because it will otherwise default to UDP
    name: https
  selector:
    app: kubia
    version: v2
---
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1 
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  annotations:
     kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx 
  name: kubia-ingress
spec:
  backend:
    serviceName: kubia-service 
    servicePort: https
  rules:
  - http:
      paths:
      - path: /api-docs
        backend:
          serviceName: kubia-service 
          servicePort: 80 
      - path: /api-status
        backend:
         serviceName: kubia-service 
         servicePort: 80
